title: gen_id() not lexicographically sortable as documented
description: id.rs documents gen_id() as 'Lexicographically sortable within a single process' but uses as_millis() which produces variable-width decimal numbers. Example: ID at timestamp 999999999999 (12 digits) sorts after 1000000000000 (13 digits) lexicographically, but represents an earlier time. After Unix epoch + ~31.7 years (year ~2001), the millisecond count crossed 10^12 and will cross 10^13 around year 2286, but the fundamental issue is that any width transition breaks sort order. Practically this won't bite until the next digit-width boundary, but the documented invariant is wrong. Fix: zero-pad the millis portion to a fixed width (e.g., 16 digits covers until year 2527).
